github.name: actions_get
github.description:
Get details about specific GitHub Actions resources.
Use this tool to get details about individual workflows, workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts by their unique IDs.

github.name: actions_list
github.description:
Tools for listing GitHub Actions resources.
Use this tool to list workflows in a repository, or list workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts for a specific workflow or workflow run.

github.name: get_code_scanning_alert
github.description: Get details of a specific code scanning alert in a GitHub repository.

github.name: get_commit
github.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

github.name: get_dependabot_alert
github.description: Get details of a specific dependabot alert in a GitHub repository.

github.name: get_discussion
github.description: Get a specific discussion by ID

github.name: get_discussion_comments
github.description: Get comments from a discussion

github.name: get_file_contents
github.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

github.name: get_gist
github.description: Get gist content of a particular gist, by gist ID

github.name: get_global_security_advisory
github.description: Get a global security advisory

github.name: get_job_logs
github.description:
Get logs for GitHub Actions workflow jobs.
Use this tool to retrieve logs for a specific job or all failed jobs in a workflow run.
For single job logs, provide job_id. For all failed jobs in a run, provide run_id with failed_only=true.

github.name: get_label
github.description: Get a specific label from a repository.

github.name: get_latest_release
github.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository

github.name: get_me
github.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user. Use this when a request is about the user's own profile for GitHub. Or when information is missing to build other tool calls.

github.name: get_notification_details
github.description: Get detailed information for a specific GitHub notification, always call this tool when the user asks for details about a specific notification, if you don't know the ID list notifications first.

github.name: get_release_by_tag
github.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository

github.name: get_repository_tree
github.description: Get the tree structure (files and directories) of a GitHub repository at a specific ref or SHA

github.name: get_secret_scanning_alert
github.description: Get details of a specific secret scanning alert in a GitHub repository.

github.name: get_tag
github.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository

github.name: get_team_members
github.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

github.name: get_teams
github.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

github.name: issue_read
github.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

github.name: list_branches
github.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

github.name: list_code_scanning_alerts
github.description: List code scanning alerts in a GitHub repository.

github.name: list_commits
github.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

github.name: list_dependabot_alerts
github.description: List dependabot alerts in a GitHub repository.

github.name: list_discussion_categories
github.description: List discussion categories with their id and name, for a repository or organisation.

github.name: list_discussions
github.description: List discussions for a repository or organisation.

github.name: list_gists
github.description: List gists for a user

github.name: list_global_security_advisories
github.description: List global security advisories from GitHub.

github.name: list_issue_types
github.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

github.name: list_issues
github.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

github.name: list_label
github.description: List labels from a repository

github.name: list_notifications
github.description: Lists all GitHub notifications for the authenticated user, including unread notifications, mentions, review requests, assignments, and updates on issues or pull requests. Use this tool whenever the user asks what to work on next, requests a summary of their GitHub activity, wants to see pending reviews, or needs to check for new updates or tasks. This tool is the primary way to discover actionable items, reminders, and outstanding work on GitHub. Always call this tool when asked what to work on next, what is pending, or what needs attention in GitHub.

github.name: list_org_repository_security_advisories
github.description: List repository security advisories for a GitHub organization.

github.name: list_pull_requests
github.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

github.name: list_releases
github.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

github.name: list_repository_security_advisories
github.description: List repository security advisories for a GitHub repository.

github.name: list_secret_scanning_alerts
github.description: List secret scanning alerts in a GitHub repository.

github.name: list_starred_repositories
github.description: List starred repositories

github.name: list_tags
github.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

github.name: projects_get
github.description:
Get details about specific GitHub Projects resources.
Use this tool to get details about individual projects, project fields, and project items by their unique IDs.

github.name: projects_list
github.description:
Tools for listing GitHub Projects resources.
Use this tool to list projects for a user or organization, or list project fields and items for a specific project.

github.name: pull_request_read
github.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

github.name: search_code
github.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

github.name: search_issues
github.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

github.name: search_orgs
github.description: Find GitHub organizations by name, location, or other organization metadata. Ideal for discovering companies, open source foundations, or teams.

github.name: search_pull_requests
github.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

github.name: search_repositories
github.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

github.name: search_users
github.description: Find GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information. Useful for locating developers, contributors, or team members.

safeoutputs.name: create_issue
safeoutputs.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 4 issue(s) can be created. Title will be prefixed with "[Perf Improver] ". Labels ["automation" "performance"] will be automatically added.

safeoutputs.name: add_comment
safeoutputs.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

safeoutputs.name: create_pull_request
safeoutputs.description: Create a new GitHub pull request to propose code changes. Use this after making file edits to submit them for review and merging. The PR will be created from the current branch with your committed changes. For code review comments on an existing PR, use create_pull_request_review_comment instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 4 pull request(s) can be created. Title will be prefixed with "[Perf Improver] ". Labels ["automation" "performance"] will be automatically added. PRs will be created as drafts.

safeoutputs.name: update_issue
safeoutputs.description: Update an existing GitHub issue's status, title, or body. Use this to modify issue properties after creation. Only the fields you specify will be updated; other fields remain unchanged. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be updated. Target: *. The target issue title must start with "[Perf Improver] ".

safeoutputs.name: push_to_pull_request_branch
safeoutputs.description: Push committed changes to a pull request's branch. Use this to add follow-up commits to an existing PR, such as addressing review feedback or fixing issues. Changes must be committed locally before calling this tool. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 4 push(es) can be made. The target pull request title must start with "[Perf Improver] ".

safeoutputs.name: missing_tool
safeoutputs.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

safeoutputs.name: noop
safeoutputs.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

safeoutputs.name: missing_data
safeoutputs.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

safeoutputs.name: push_repo_memory
safeoutputs.description: Validate repo-memory files are within configured size limits before the workflow completes. Call this after writing files to memory to check that the total size is within limits. Returns an error if files are too large, with guidance on how to reduce memory size so the memory can be saved successfully.
